pages.full_text:
Simon thinks the swimming is too dangerous for John & Charles. We went out to __Restaurant des Grottes__ & had pâté, lamb & floating island or Oeufs dans la neige.
__August 5th Friday.__
      We reached __Vézelay__. It's on a hill. Glorious. 5 different carvers. Great skill in showing movement & skin, muscle & bone. A lovely long old street to walk up cf Conques. It was the start of pilgrimages to Compostella. St. Bernard preached a crusade. The Tympanum has a Christ who welcomes pilgrims, seeming to move out towards them. They all look happy & excited. I pointed out David & Goliath to Thomas & William. Simon & I saw Lazarus & Dives & the mystied mill. Moses, OThaw = grain. Christ is mill & Apostles.
      William has spent ages negotiating hair gel, instructions & sale – 5p a squirt to Peter. John & Thomas went to fetch bread. Charles has bought Duplo.

[hand-drawn sketch of a hillside landscape with a building silhouette]
View from lunch picnic on a hill after leaving Vézelay.
pages.route_sketch_description: A simple pen sketch of a hillside landscape with what appears to be a building or structure on the hill, captioned 'View from lunch picnic on a hill after leaving Vézelay.'
pages.notes: 'mystied mill' may be 'mystic mill' — a carved tympanum motif at Vézelay. 'OThaw = grain' — uncertain reading, possibly 'OThaw' is a name or abbreviation for something in the iconography. Date year is uncertain: August 5th falling on a Friday fits 1994 among years in the 1989–2003 range, but the year cannot be confirmed from text alone — the confidence is therefore moderate.

pages.full_text:
[glued-in black and white photograph of a stone carved relief showing two figures with a millstone/wheel between them, Romanesque style]

We drove 40 minutes south & the campsite where we had wanted to stay, by a big lake, was crowded, so we came back along the winding road to [?hormes]. * * * *
      The campsite is at the end of a long, ugly
photo_captions: [photograph of Romanesque stone carving, two figures with a wheel/millstone — likely from Vézelay tympanum]
pages.notes: The place name after 'winding road to' reads '[?hormes]' — could be a village name; possibly 'Lormes', 'Mormes', or another place near Vézelay in Burgundy. The text is cut off mid-sentence ('long, ugly') at the bottom of the page — continues on the next spread. The asterisks '* * * *' appear to be a section break or editorial mark.
